decisions.decision: Expand HTTP-layer resolution test with full event payload assertions to match direct-layer parity (marginalDelta, pricing, replayStateHash, resolution.outcomeId, resolution.finalProbabilities, eventType)
alternatives_rejected: Leave HTTP test as-is since direct layer already covers event payload — rejected because the task explicitly requires parity between direct and HTTP layers

decisions.decision: Add a direct-layer test for already-resolved market rejection (409/market_already_resolved) via route_request, mirroring the existing HTTP-layer test
alternatives_rejected: Skip direct-layer rejection test since HTTP layer covers it — rejected because the task requires coverage at both layers

decisions.decision: Strengthen idempotent replay test to compare event payload fields (resolution, effects, pricing, replayStateHash) between first and replayed responses
alternatives_rejected: Full response body deep-equal — rejected because request echo may structurally differ due to cross-shape idempotency (legacy vs finalProbabilities body)

decisions.decision: Leave closed-market test (line 3198) as-is — it is intentionally minimal since no orders/risk/exposure exist to settle
alternatives_rejected: Expand closed-market test with settlement assertions — rejected because that would require adding order/risk setup, creating a fundamentally different test

decisions.decision: No changes needed for risk doc retention assertions (q1) or single-market resolution (q3) — both are already functionally covered
alternatives_rejected: Add a dedicated single-market-only resolution test — rejected because the HTTP test already covers sole-risk-market resolution with markets={}, slices={}

steps.task: Expand test_market_resolve_http_settles_account_exposure (around line 8183) with event payload assertions: assert eventType, resolution.outcomeId, resolution.finalProbabilities, effects.marginalDelta (full array with per-outcome deltas), pricing (cost=0, fee=0), and replayStateHash. Follow the same assertion pattern used in the direct-layer test at lines 3092-3130.

Add a new direct-layer test method test_market_resolution_rejects_already_resolved near the existing rejection tests (around line 3261). Use route_request to resolve m3 (pre-resolved in setUp), assert ApiError with status 409 and code 'market_already_resolved'. Follow the pattern from the HTTP test at line 8051.

Strengthen the idempotent replay test (line 3239) to compare event payload fields between first and replayed responses. After the existing ID/replayed assertions, extract the event from both responses and assert that resolution, effects, and pricing fields match. replayStateHash should also match.

decomposition_assessment.decision_reason: Total estimated lines (70) is well under 500. Zero high-risk steps. All changes are in a single file (tests/test_bayes_market_api.py) following established assertion patterns, making parallel execution impractical and unnecessary. All three steps are independent but share the same file, so sequential execution in one session is optimal.
